message:
feat(core): add ImeEvent and ImePhase types to the event model

Introduce first-class IME (Input Method Editor) composition support in
the core event enum. Previously, composition text was shoehorned into
PasteEvent at the web input parser layer, losing all intermediate
composition lifecycle state (start, update, cancel) and only surfacing
the final committed text. This made it impossible for widgets to show
preedit underlines, manage composition-aware cursor positions, or
cancel in-flight composition gracefully.

New types:
- ImePhase enum with Start, Update, Commit, and Cancel variants
  representing the full IME composition lifecycle.
- ImeEvent struct carrying phase + text payload, with ergonomic
  constructors (start(), update(preedit), commit(text), cancel()).
- Event::Ime variant wired into the existing event discriminant
  with kind_name() returning "ime".

Tests cover all four constructor helpers and verify the new variant
integrates into existing event construction smoke tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
